Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

We should avoid writing pairs of functions like hur_len_okay() and hur_len() where we need to call the first to see if it's OK to call the second. Can the overflow checks be moved into hur_len() and converted to error returns?

There is no reason for hur_len() to return int. size_t is the correct return type.

Also both functions should take a const struct hsm_user_request *hur.

parents.subject: LU-4018 tests: improve racer file_create workload

commit.subject: LU-4984 llite: check for integer overflow in hsm user request
commit.message:
LU-4984 llite: check for integer overflow in hsm user request

Check to make sure total size of request does not overflow when
calculated.  Return -1 from hur_len() if it does overflow.

Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Change-Id: I6a3ca50c53ff38dab4165f20dac6fa00eb888df2
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10615
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
